# HarvestMath

> Free, mobile-friendly gardening calculators covering soil, mulch, fertilizer, watering, spacing, lawns, trees, indoor plants, hydroponics, and greenhouses — no sign-up required.

HarvestMath.com provides calculators for home gardeners, landscapers, and houseplant owners, organized into 8 clusters. Each calculator page includes a worked example, common mistakes, and an FAQ section. All calculators run entirely in the browser — no data is sent to a server.

## Calculators

- [Topsoil Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/topsoil-calculator/): calculates topsoil volume in cubic feet, cubic yards, tons, and bags
- [Raised Bed Soil Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/raised-bed-soil-calculator/): soil volume and mix ratio for raised beds, with common size presets
- [Soil Volume Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/soil-volume-calculator/): soil volume for rectangular beds and cylindrical pots/containers
- [Mulch Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/mulch-calculator/): mulch volume for rectangular and circular beds
- [Plant Spacing Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/plant-spacing-calculator/): plant count and layout by crop, row or square-foot method
- [Grass Seed Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/grass-seed-calculator/): seed quantity by grass type, new lawn or overseeding
- [Overseeding Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/overseeding-calculator/): seed and topdressing amounts to overseed an existing lawn, by grass type and condition
- [Tree Age Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/tree-age-calculator/): estimates tree age from trunk circumference without cutting it down
- [NPK Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/npk-calculator/): fertilizer application rate, liquid dilution, and multi-fertilizer blending
- [Pot Size Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/pot-size-calculator/): pot size conversion, repotting sizing, and container guide by plant
- [Frost Date Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/frost-date-calculator/): estimated frost dates and planting timeline by hardiness zone
- [Compost Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/compost-calculator/): compost volume, bags, and weight for beds and top-dressing
- [Soil Type Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/soil-type-calculator/): classifies soil texture using the USDA soil triangle, from sand/silt/clay percentages or jar test measurements
- [Seed Starting Calendar](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/seed-starting-calculator/): personalized seed-starting and transplant schedule by frost date
- [Drip Irrigation Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/drip-irrigation-calculator/): run time and water delivered based on emitter flow rate
- [Rain Barrel Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/rain-barrel-calculator/): rainwater collection from roof area and rainfall, plus spigot water pressure
- [USDA Hardiness Zone Finder](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/hardiness-zone-finder/): find your exact USDA plant hardiness zone by ZIP code, plus the average annual minimum winter temperature range it represents
- [Grow Light & DLI Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/grow-light-calculator/): grow light coverage, wattage, DLI, and hanging distance
- [Vegetable Yield Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/vegetable-yield-calculator/): estimated harvest yield by crop and area
- [Greenhouse Heater Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/greenhouse-heater-calculator/): estimated heat loss and recommended heater size (BTU) by shape, glazing, and climate
- [Hydroponic Nutrient Calculator](https://harvestmath.com/calculators/hydroponic-nutrient-calculator/): scale any nutrient product's label dose by reservoir size and growth stage, plus EC-to-PPM conversion on the 500/640/700 scales

## Guides

- [How to Read and Understand Your Soil Test Results](https://harvestmath.com/blog/how-to-read-soil-test-results/): what pH, organic matter, N-P-K, and micronutrient readings on a soil test actually mean, and how to turn them into a fertilizer plan
- [Succession Planting: How to Get More Harvests From the Same Space](https://harvestmath.com/blog/succession-planting/): the three real succession planting methods (staggered sowing, staggered-maturity varieties, interplanting) with a worked raised-bed example
- [8 Fertilizing Mistakes That Are Hurting Your Garden and Lawn](https://harvestmath.com/blog/8-fertilizing-mistakes-hurting-your-garden-and-lawn/): the most common fertilizing mistakes, from over-application to bad timing, and how to avoid and fix each one
- [How to Build a Rainwater Collection System for Your Garden](https://harvestmath.com/blog/rainwater-collection-system/): step-by-step rain barrel setup covering downspout connection, first-flush filtering, overflow routing, and winterizing
- [8 Mulch Mistakes That Are Secretly Hurting Your Plants](https://harvestmath.com/blog/8-mulch-mistakes-hurting-your-plants/): the most common mulching mistakes, from volcano mulching to guessing the amount, and how to avoid each one
- [Cool-Season vs. Warm-Season Grass: Which Type Do You Have?](https://harvestmath.com/blog/cool-season-vs-warm-season-grass/): the C3/C4 photosynthesis difference behind cool-season and warm-season grass, how to tell which you have, and what to plant in the transition zone
- [Topsoil vs. Garden Soil: What’s the Difference?](https://harvestmath.com/blog/topsoil-vs-garden-soil/): explains how topsoil and garden soil differ and which one to use for a given project

## Reference

- [Methodology](https://harvestmath.com/methodology/): how calculator formulas are sourced and verified
- [About](https://harvestmath.com/about/): who runs HarvestMath and why
